cards.back: The unfair or discriminatory treatment of certain groups of people in AI systems, which can perpetuate existing social inequalities.
The practice of making AI decision-making processes and data used in those decisions accessible to users, to understand how decisions are being made.
The principle of ensuring that AI systems are free from bias and discrimination, and provide accurate and unbiased results for all users.
A controlled environment where startups and innovators can test and deploy new AI technologies, with minimal regulatory oversight, to encourage innovation and experimentation.
The practice of making AI decision-making processes and results transparent and understandable, so that users can understand how decisions were made.
